channel.title: AI Security on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in AI Security on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: security: ​​Microsoft named an overall leader in KuppingerCole Analyst’s 2026 Emerging AI Security Operations Center (SOC) report ​​
item.description:
Microsoft says KuppingerCole Analyst named it an Overall Leader and Market Leader in the 2026 Emerging AI Security Operations Center (SOC) report. The post frames automation and AI as central to future cybersecurity.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Applying security fundamentals to AI: Practical advice for CISOs
item.description:
Microsoft Security published guidance for CISOs on securing AI systems by applying established security fundamentals to AI-powered environments. The post focuses on practical steps for managing AI risk rather than introducing new product features.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Detecting and analyzing prompt abuse in AI tools
item.description:
Microsoft describes how hidden instructions in content can influence AI tool behavior and uses a scenario to illustrate prompt injection. The post emphasizes the need for human oversight and a structured response playbook.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Scaling security operations with Microsoft Defender autonomous defense and expert-led services
item.description:
Guide-style post arguing that manual SOC processes and legacy tools can’t keep up with AI-enabled attacks, and positioning Microsoft Defender’s autonomous capabilities plus expert-led services as the proposed approach.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog
